In the section on social proof, we've already considered how multiple positive reviews or testimonials for your product create the kind of social proof that will influence others in your direction. But social proof isn't the only principle of influence that is engaged by positive testimonials. The principle of authority can be activated as well, provided the testimonial comes from an acknowledged expert on the topic. I can provide a personal example. After the new edition of my book Influence came out, it received a lot of reviews from readers saying that they had used it to improve their business performance. Then, when it had already received hundreds of positive reviews from such everyday people, the founder of the e-commerce sensation Shopify, Tobias Ludke, gave it a rave review on the Tim Ferriss podcast, crediting it with teaching him the skills to build a billion-dollar business as well as a multi-billion-dollar net worth. That day, the book's ranking on Amazon went from number 103 to number 3, so on that day at least, one widely respected authority testifier was more effective than hundreds of unknown testifiers regarding the book's value. It's another remarkable example of the power of being seen as an authority on the topic at hand.
